content.claim: As of June 2026, Education Minister Lannert Judit acknowledged the admissions system produces significant social selection and places heavy burden on children and families, explicitly denying a plan to abolish tracks outright but proposing societal dialogue on admissions-mechanism reform; Fidesz faction opposition politically reframed procedural review as existential abolition threat.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The statement reflects ministerial position, not legislative or constitutional authority to reform
Political reframing indicates that even technical admissions changes face polarized interpretation, constraining what reforms are politically feasible
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
